content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of NNN integers and an integer KKK. Find the number of (**possibly empty**) subsequences of AAA such that no two elements in the subsequence have a sum that is divisible by KKK.

Two subsequences are considered distinct if they are made up of different indices. For example, A=[1,2,1,2]A = [1, 2, 1, 2]A=[1,2,1,2] contains [1,2][1, 2][1,2] as a subsequence three times, and all 333 must be counted separately in the answer.

More formally,


Let S={x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xm}S = \{x_1, x_2, \ldots, x_m\}S={x1‚Äã,x2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,xm‚Äã} be a (possibly empty) set such that 1‚â§x1<x2<‚Ä¶<xm‚â§N1 \leq x_1 \lt x_2 \lt \ldots \lt x_m \leq N1‚â§x1‚Äã<x2‚Äã<‚Ä¶<xm‚Äã‚â§N.
SSS is said to be good if Axi+AxjA_{x_i} + A_{x_j}Axi‚Äã‚Äã+Axj‚Äã‚Äã is not divisible by KKK for any 1‚â§i<j‚â§m1 \leq i \lt j \leq m1‚â§i<j‚â§m. In particular, any subsequence of size ‚â§1\leq 1‚â§1 is good.
Your task is to count the number of good sets.
Since the answer can be very large, print it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space separated integers, NNN and KKK.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line number of subsequences satisfying the condition, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: The sum of NNN and KKK over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Here, K=4K = 4K=4. There are 888 subsequences of the array, and they are considered as follows:

[][][]: the empty subsequence. It satisfies the condition.
[4],[5],[7][4], [5], [7][4],[5],[7]: size 111 subsequences, all are good.
[4,5][4, 5][4,5]: good, because 4+5=94+5 = 94+5=9 is not a multiple of 444
[4,7][4, 7][4,7]: good, because 4+7=114+7 = 114+7=11 is not a multiple of 444
[5,7][5, 7][5,7]: not good, because 5+7=125+7=125+7=12 is a multiple of 444.
[4,5,7][4, 5, 7][4,5,7]: not good, again because it contains 555 and 777.

So, 666 of the subsequences are good.
